Ethnic dance groups were a highlight of the current dance program. These groups performed in various special programs throughout the year and at the Stern Grove Carnival as well. Folk and Square Dance Classes are regularly programmed for senior citizens and the Golden Gate Park Senior Center Dance Group presented a fine program for the annual Hobby Show and The Emporium. In drama, groups meriting special recognition were conducted at Cayuga, Eureka Valley,

gue Convention was held at Del Webb's Towne House June 30th-July 3rd. Over 200 delegates from all over the country were hosted by the Golden Gate and Storyland Story Leagues sponsored by this division. -9- -2- Annual city-wide programs organized and conducted by this Division were: Carnival at Stern Grove (a Recreation pageant with a cast of 1500 children and adults); May Day Festival at Kezar Stadium, Golden Gate Park; Drama Expression Days at Chinese Center, Hamilton, St. Mary's, Sunset and Upper Noe Playgrounds; Folk Dance Program for Bernal Heights Association Art Fair and Festival; Doll Show at

us Day pageant. Our costume section provides costumes and properties for many special holiday programs throughout the year when called upon by directors and also provides: day camp scarves (making, repairing and laundry service - approximately 400 a week used during summer camp season) tablecloths for Stern Grove Summer Concert Series, drapes for dedications and special occasions, repairing flags and dressing room tents where possible, and ground cloths for bedding plants. By attending meetings, serving on committees and assembling material, assistance was given to other divisions of the Recreation and Park Depa

was originally intended as a one-time event in the Fall of 1965, but subsequently was repeated on a more and more frequent basis until the present weekly schedule was established. st in photography as a recreational hobby in San Francisco. The Photography Center also sponsors and conducts one of the largest photographic events in California, namely, "Photography Day". The Pine Lake Park Improvement Plan is a $36 million, 10-year improvement plan to be realized in six phases, as funds become available. Stern Grove and Pine Lake Park Improvement Project Project Update December 2005 Rhoda Goldman Memorial Concert Meadow 'llie newly renovated Concert Meadow finishes the 2005 concert season with resounding success! The first phase of the Park Improvement Plan was completed in June 2005, through a generous gift to the city by the Stern Grove Festival Association. The renovation of the Rhoda Goldman Memorial Concert Meadow included new grass terrace seating and new stage and backstage buildings. As part of that renovation, improvements were made to the entrance at 1 9th Avenue and Sloat Boulevard to improve pedestrian and vehicular circulation and formali2e the park's main entrance. CA 94102 REFERENCE BOOK Not to be taken from the Library •lOAT lOUltVARO Prepared by: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCIS

ting Services, Inc., Lakes: George Fullerton May Consultants, Wildlife Habitat: Loran May The Duffy Company, Lakes & Wildlife Habitat: Michele Bellows SCA Consultants, Hazardous Materials: Chucl< Siu URS Corporation, Erosion Control: Tom Sweet, Jil Finnegan 250" 1?^/ 0 500' SCALE: 1" = 2W-0" STERN GROVE AND PINE LAKE PARK IMPROVEMENT PLAN RECOMMENDATIONS REPORT Table of Contents TABLE OF CONTENTS CONCEPTUAL n F y i g N RgPORT 1.0 Introduction Table of Contents Introduction • Planning Process • Park History • Natural History, Character, Function and Use 2.0 Conceptual Design Plan First

Contents 8.0 Maintenance and Operations Maintenance and Operations 8.1 9.0 Park Management and Programming Park l^anagement 9.1 Park Programming 9.2 10.0 Implementation and Phasing Funding Needs and Phasing of Improvements 10.1 PInasing Strategy 10.2 11.0 Acknowledgements Hi Table of Contents STERN GROVE & PINE LAKE PARK INTRODUCTION The Need for Improvements The Improvement Plan Process Executive Summary 6^ RECOMMENDATIONS REPORT Introduction INTRODUCTION THE NEED FOR IMPROVEMENTS A Unique Resource stern Grove and Pine Lake Park together present one of the most unique park settings within the City of San Francisco. With a total of 64 acres, the parks function as one facility and provide a range of opportunities for recreational and cultural activities. The size of the park alone allows for an uncommon

w the street-level entrances of the surrounding neighborhood, this separation from the city is dramatically enhanced. The park's rustic character has distinguished the park landscape as a setting for activities not usually offered in the midst of a dense urban environment. The land which now comprises Stern Grove and Pine Lake Park has been used for recreational and leisure activities for over a century. * Today, the parks are used as a popular facility for walking and hiking, dog-play, picnicking, and a variety of active uses such as tennis, croquet, and horseshoes. Most notably, the Rhoda Goldman Concert Meadow in Stern Grove provides a dramatic setting for Sunday summer concerts, which offer world-class performances free to the public. These free concerts began in 1938, not long after Rosalie M. Stern deeded her property over to the City, with the provision that it
